response.summary: The slice does not meet the UX contract in the current user-facing app because the dedicated vaccination area is not actually exposed to users. An isolated vaccination component exists, but the live flow only mounts onboarding and plan surfaces. In addition, visible success copy uses mechanical medical-record framing instead of preventive tracking language.

findings.observed: The current app only renders onboarding and plan views. The vaccination tracking area component is not routed or linked from the active user flow, so users cannot reach a dedicated Vaccinations screen with its own due guidance and add-entry action.

The confirmation copy 'Vaccination entry saved and added to your records.' emphasizes system storage and broad record-keeping. That wording is less aligned with the slice’s preventive tracking purpose and risks implying a medical-record experience.
